resolution: health-with-findings' SSH section (3 findings) then Git section (2 findings), each severity-sorted critical -> error -> warning -> info, appears in the SAME order in both media; health-all-green's SSH-then-Git order matches; finding-detail's severity/family-chip-then-explanation-then-suggested-fix order matches; per-identity-health's SSH-note-then-Git-finding-then-Manager-handoff-note order matches; parse-error's file-then-raw-error-then-snippet-then-explanation order matches. Verified via html/*.png and tui/*.png side-by-side review (FIELDS.md) and TestHealth_EveryScreenRendersNonEmptyWithSignatureAndBreadcrumb (Go).

Labels and helper copy match verbatim between media wherever both are present: the read-only banner text, all 5 findings' titles/explanations/suggested-fixes, the health-all-green SSH/Git summary sentences, the finding-detail contradiction explanation, the per-identity-health SSH note and Manager-handoff sentence, and the parse-error raw error/snippet/explanation are all byte-identical (recipeFixtures.ts's health* exports vs surface_health.go's hlth* Go constants). ONE accepted exception: health-with-findings' master-detail right-pane preview (the highlighted finding's full explanation) is TUI-omitted in favor of a one-line keybar hint (viewport compaction, CRITIQUE.md finding #2) while present in HTML -- logged in FIELDS.md row 6 and this surface's field-set-and-order row, not a labels-and-helper-copy-verbatim finding because the omission is a live-PTY layout constraint, not a wording mismatch (the same text is verbatim, one keystroke away, on finding-detail in both media).

The 5-finding set (2 critical/error on SSH, 1 warning on SSH, 1 error on Git, 1 info on Git) appears with the same severity, family, and title in both media (health-with-findings.png html/tui pair; TestHealth_ContradictionAndRedundancyExamplesPresent, TestHealth_FourSeverityLevelsPresentWithLockedGlyphContract). The LOCKED severity-glyph contract (warning=! yellow, error/critical=✗ red, info=~ cyan, never ✗ for warning) matches exactly between media -- verified via direct color/glyph comparison of the screenshot pairs and the Go negative assertion.

The concrete examples are recipe-faithful: the IdentitiesOnly contradiction targets a Host block shape matching recipes/ssh-config.recipe's per-alias convention (IdentityFile + IdentitiesOnly on one Host block); the duplicate Host * finding references the SAME Host * stanza shape global-ssh/global-git's own fixtures use (recipes/ssh-config.recipe's Host * block, guarded by IgnoreUnknown UseKeychain); the includeIf-targets-a-missing-fragment finding uses the recipes/gitconfig.recipe includeIf shape (gitdir: match). All three are byte-identical across recipeFixtures.ts's healthFindings and surface_health.go's hlthFindings.

All 5 named states from UX-DIRECTION.md §4.6 exist as distinct, navigable screens in both media (manifest.json screen list == surface_health.go ScreenDef IDs == route file titles), reachable via the SAME transitions in both: health-with-findings (entry, key 4) -> health-all-green (h) / finding-detail (v) / per-identity-health (i) / parse-error (x), all one hop from the entry screen. Verified by TestHealth_KeysFormAConnectedPathReachingEveryScreen (Go), manual html/tui screenshot review, and the surface-scoped dummy-nav e2e (TestDummyNavReachesAllScreens/health) reaching every screen through the running binary.

This surface's ONLY safety affordance is the ABSENCE of the mutation ceremony (§5): health has zero confirm/backup/apply beats by design (§4.6's read-only-integrity highest-risk affordance), and that absence is consistent across BOTH media on all 5 screens -- verified by TestHealth_NoWriteCeremonyMarkerAnywhere (Go, negative assertion) and direct screenshot review (no confirm/backup/apply UI element appears in any of the 10 captured PNGs). See the dedicated read-only-integrity row below for the positive/negative assertion detail.

Every intra-flow key (h/v/i/x) is shown in the keybar/footer of the screen where it applies in BOTH media (Keybar.tsx entries vs renderShellKeybar output); health's keys allocate h=health-all-green/v=finding-detail/i=per-identity-health/x=parse-error, all fresh letters, never n/g (create-flow's/git-screen's own LaunchKeys -- TestHealth_IntraSurfaceKeysNeverReuseLaunchKeysNOrG); reserved keys (Esc/q/?//) are consistent across every screenshot pair reviewed. See CRITIQUE.md finding #3 for the one accepted label-WORDING difference (TUI shows the raw target screen ID, HTML shows a semantic action phrase -- the key itself and its destination match), the same class already accepted for every prior fan-out surface.

HLTH-01: health-with-findings, health-all-green, and per-identity-health each carry a distinct 'SSH' section heading followed by a 'Git' section heading, never merged, in both media -- verified by TestHealth_ListAndSummaryScreensHaveBothSSHAndGitSections (Go) and direct screenshot-pair review. finding-detail and parse-error are deliberate single-finding deep-dives (the same detail-screen pattern global-ssh/global-git's own option-detail already establishes) -- each still NAMES which section its one finding belongs to (finding-detail shows 'SSH / Coherence'; parse-error shows 'Git -- ~/.gitconfig.d/work'), verified by TestHealth_DetailScreensNameTheirOwnSection (Go).

§4.6/§5 highest-risk affordance: the explicit 'Health only diagnoses -- nothing here writes to your files. Open the Fixer (key 5) to change anything shown.' banner (healthReadOnlyNote/hlthReadOnlyNote, byte-identical) appears on ALL 5 screens in BOTH media, in the same position (directly under the heading) -- verified by TestHealth_ReadOnlyIntegrityBannerOnEveryScreen (Go, positive assertion) and direct screenshot review (all 10 PNGs). The NEGATIVE check (review LOW-11) additionally confirms NO confirm/backup/apply write-ceremony marker string (e.g. 'Confirm write', 'Backup created', 'Options applied', 'confirm-write', 'backup-notice', 'result-applied') appears anywhere in the combined rendered output of the TUI dummy (TestHealth_NoWriteCeremonyMarkerAnywhere) nor in any route file or FIELDS.md (the route-file/filename grep checks from Task 1's acceptance criteria). Every finding's suggestedFix names the hand-off to the Fixer screen (key 5) rather than offering an in-place action -- the fix ITSELF never appears on this surface.
